channel_title: AI News & Strategy Daily | Nate B Jones
title: Cheating at Cheating? Cluely's $120M Bet on Proactive AI
executive_summary: Cluely, a proactive AI tool, has been valued at $120 million by A16z, not for its raw intelligence but for its ability to create habitual workflows. The tool markets itself as a 'cheat tool' to attract attention while providing an invisible overlay that assists users across applications. This innovative approach is designed to resonate with the first AI-native generation, particularly Gen-Z.
bullets: Cluely positions itself as a 'cheat tool' to attract headlines and free attention, masking its true innovation. This strategy is like a magician who distracts the audience with flashy tricks while the real magic happens behind the scenes.

The term 'level-two proactive agent' describes Cluely's functionality, which includes an invisible overlay that tracks user activity across apps. Imagine having a personal assistant who anticipates your needs before you even ask, making your daily tasks smoother and more efficient.

Investors valued Cluely at $120 million because habitual workflows are more important than raw model power. This is akin to choosing a reliable car that gets you to work every day over a flashy sports car that’s fun but impractical for daily use.

Cluely's team consists of seven marketers and four engineers, targeting the first AI-native generation, Gen-Z. This is like a band of musicians who know exactly how to play the right notes to capture the hearts of a new audience.

The user experience (UX) of Cluely is described as brilliant, even if the underlying model is considered mid-level. This is similar to a beautifully designed smartphone that feels great to use, even if the technology inside isn't the latest and greatest.

Cluely has already secured seven-figure deals in the enterprise sector, demonstrating its effectiveness in customer success and sales environments. Think of it like a popular restaurant that consistently attracts large crowds because of its great service and atmosphere.

The quote 'Cluely isn’t a cheating tool; it’s a level-two proactive AI agent dressed in clickbait' emphasizes the tool's true purpose. This highlights how marketing can sometimes mislead, much like a movie trailer that shows only the most exciting parts of a film.

The concept of 'inflationary cheating' suggests that once everyone has access to tools like Cluely, the competitive edge diminishes. This is like a classroom where every student has the same textbook; the real advantage will come from how well they understand and apply the material.

Cluely's model is compared to ChatGPT-4o, indicating that while it may not be the most advanced, its practical application is what matters. It’s like comparing a solid, dependable family car to a high-performance race car; both serve different purposes.

The video discusses how distribution strategy trumps raw intelligence, meaning that how a product is delivered and adopted is more critical than its technical capabilities. This is similar to how a great book can become a bestseller not just because of its content but also due to effective marketing and distribution.

Cluely's approach to marketing and functionality is designed to create a habit loop for users, making it an integral part of their daily routine. This is like a morning coffee ritual that becomes essential for starting the day right.

The speaker notes that ethical guardrails will shape tool choice, indicating that users must consider the implications of their tools. Think of it like choosing a vehicle; you wouldn’t want to drive one that’s unsafe or has a bad reputation.
key_takeaways: Focus on building habitual workflows in your AI projects. This matters because consistent user engagement leads to long-term success, and without it, your tool may be forgotten like a one-hit wonder.
By the end of the year, ensure your product has a user experience that stands out, even if the underlying technology is average. If you skip this, you risk losing users to competitors with better UX.

Set a goal to secure at least one seven-figure deal in the enterprise sector within the next six months. This is crucial because it validates your product's effectiveness and can significantly boost your company's reputation.

technical_terms.level-two proactive agent: An advanced AI tool that anticipates user needs by monitoring their activities across applications. Think of it like a smart assistant who knows your schedule and reminds you of tasks before you even think of them.
technical_terms.habit loop: A cycle that encourages users to engage with a product regularly, creating a routine. Like brushing your teeth every morning, it becomes a necessary part of your day.
technical_terms.UX (User Experience): The overall experience a user has when interacting with a product, including ease of use and satisfaction. Think of it like the comfort of a well-designed chair; if it feels good, you’ll want to sit in it more often.
technical_terms.inflationary cheating: The phenomenon where the competitive advantage of using a tool diminishes as everyone gains access to it. Like a popular recipe that everyone learns, it loses its uniqueness.
technical_terms.distribution strategy: The method by which a product is delivered and marketed to users, crucial for its adoption. Think of it like a well-planned party; if the invitations are sent out properly, everyone will show up.
